name: Rice Blast
symptoms: Diamond-shaped lesions on leaves with gray center and brown border
White or gray patches on panicles (neck rot)
Rotting at panicle neck causing empty grains

organic: Silica-rich amendments to strengthen cell walls
chemical: Spray at boot leaf and heading stage
preventive: Avoid excess nitrogen application
Use resistant varieties: CO-51, Jyothi, Haritha
Drain water from field when disease appears

name: Brown Plant Hopper (BPH)
symptoms: Circular 'hopper burn' patches of yellowing/deadening
Honeydew deposits on leaf sheath
Insects visible at base of plant when disturbed
conditions: Dense crop canopy, excess nitrogen, continuous flooding

organic: Set up light traps (1/ha) during 6–10 PM
chemical: Direct spray at base of plant
preventive: Avoid excess nitrogen
Drain fields for 5-7 days at tillering
Grow resistant varieties: Bala, Bhadra, Jyothi

name: Phytophthora Foot Rot (Quick Wilt)
symptoms: Sudden wilting and collapse of the vine
Blackening at collar region of vine
Dark brown rot spreading upward
Berries and leaves fall rapidly
conditions: Waterlogged soil, monsoon season, poor drainage

organic: Drench base with Trichoderma viride (10g/litre) before monsoon
Apply neem cake 200g per vine to soil
chemical: Soil drench with Bordeaux mixture (1%) or Metalaxyl-M + Mancozeb
Copper oxychloride spray on collar region
preventive: Ensure proper drainage around pepper vines
Apply Trichoderma enriched compost before monsoon
Never allow water to stagnate at base
Roguing and removal of infected vines

name: Rhinoceros Beetle
symptoms: V-shaped cuts on unopened fronds
Holes in crown region of palm
Damage to spadix and nuts
conditions: Piles of organic matter, decaying logs nearby

organic: Baculovirus oryctes (10ml/litre) injected into crown holes
Hook out and kill beetles from crown
Apply Beauveria bassiana in crown and compost pits
chemical: Mix carbofuran granules (5g) with sand and place in crown
Swab crown with coal tar + kerosene (1:2)
preventive: Remove decaying wood from near palms
Fill crown with sand + naphthalene balls
Set up pheromone traps

name: Banana Bunchy Top Virus
symptoms: Leaves narrow, erect, with marginal yellowing
Dark green streaks on midrib and petioles
Plant remains stunted, no bunch formation

organic: No cure — rogue out infected plants immediately
Spray neem oil (3%) to control aphid vectors
chemical: Dimethoate 30EC @ 2ml/litre sprayed on healthy plants to kill aphids
preventive: Use virus-indexed suckers or tissue culture plants
Uproot and destroy infected plants
Do not replant suckers from infected plants
Maintain field hygiene

name: Cassava Mosaic Disease (CMD)
symptoms: Mosaic pattern (yellow-green patches) on leaves
Leaf distortion and curling
Stunted plant growth and reduced tuber yield

organic: Yellow sticky traps to trap white flies (2/0.1ha)
chemical: No chemical cure for the virus itself
preventive: Use disease-free planting material from certified nurseries
Rogue out infected plants
Use resistant varieties H-226, Sree Sahya, Sree Prakash

name: Soft Rot / Pythium Rhizome Rot
symptoms: Water-soaked, mushy rhizome at collar region
Yellowing of leaves starting from lower leaves upward
Foul smell from rotting rhizomes
Collapse of pseudostem
conditions: Waterlogged soil, high humidity, infected seed rhizomes

organic: Drench with Trichoderma harzianum (10g/litre of water)
Treat seed rhizomes with bioagents before planting
preventive: Treat seed rhizomes with Metalaxyl before planting
Ensure good drainage in beds
Avoid planting in disease-prone areas
Soil solarization before planting
